subject: Geography
title: Mountain Borders
description: A quick, practical guide to Mountain Borders.

hook.text: The Pyrenees have separated France and Spain for over a thousand years.

buildup.text: Mountains are barriers—hard to cross, easy to defend, natural dividing lines.

discovery.text: The Andes divide Chile and Argentina along 5,000 km of peaks and glaciers.

twist.text: But ridgelines aren't always clear. Chile and Argentina disputed their border for a century.

climax.text: They almost went to war in 1978 before the Pope mediated a settlement.

punchline.text: Even mountains can't stop a border dispute.

quiz.question: Who mediated the Chile-Argentina border dispute?
